text: APPENDIX 2 WORDS AND PHRASES TO BE USED IN RTF Phrase Meaning ACKNOWLEDGE Let me know. AFFIRM Yes. ALL STATIONS All aircraft stations on this frequency. APPROVED Permission for proposed action granted. BREAK I hereby indicate the separation between portions of the message. (to be used where there is no clear distinction between the text and other portions of the message). BREAK BREAK I hereby indicate the separation between massages transmitted to the different aircraft in a very busy environment. CANCEL Annul the previously transmitted clearance. CHECK Examine a system or procedure. (No answer is expected). CLEARED Authorized to proceed under the conditions specified. CONFIRM ‘Have I correctly received the message?’ or ‘Did you correctly receive the message?’ CONTACT Establish radio contact with ……………. CORRECT That is correct. CORRECTION An error has been made in this transmission (or message indicated). The correct version is ………. DISREGARD Consider that transmission as not sent. GO AHEAD Proceed with your message. HOW DO YOU READ? What is the readability of my transmission? I SAY AGAIN I repeat for clarity or emphasis. MONITOR Listen out on (frequency). NEGATIVE ‘No.’ or ‘Permission not granted’ or ‘That is not correct’. ORBIT Make a 360° turn. OUT This exchange of transmission is ended and no response is expected. OVER My transmission is ended and I expect a response from you. READ BACK Repeat all, or the specified communications part of the message back to me exactly as received. RECLEARED A change has been made to your last clearance and this new clearance supersedes your previous clearance or part thereof. REPORT Pass the following information ………………. REQUEST I should like to know or wish to obtain ……… ROGER I have received all your last transmission. SAY AGAIN Repeat all, or the following part of your last transmission. SPEAK SLOWER Reduce your rate of speech. STANDBY Wait and I will call you. UNABLE COMPLY Not able to carry out received instruction. VACATE Leave or depart from VERIFY Check and confirm with originator. WILCO Abbreviation for ‘Will comply.’ I understand your message and will comply with it.

text: APPENDIX 5 STANDARD PHRASEOLOGY FOR SSR COMMUNICATIONS PHRASE MEANING “Squawk” Operate transponder or check its operating condition “Squawk Alpha Code 1027” Select Mode A and Code 1027 “Squawk Three Code …………” Select Mode 3 and Code ……….. (IFF/SIF equipped aircraft) “Squawk Ident” Use IDENT or I/P feature, retaining present code “Squawk Alpha 7700” Select “Emergency” “Squawk Standby” Switch to Standby retaining present code “Squawk Altimeter” Switch on altimeter reporting facility “Stop Squawk” Switch off transponder “Stop Squawk Three” Switch off Mode 3 (Military aircraft only) “Stop Altimeter Squawk” Switch off altitude reporting “Recycle (Mode) (Code) To request the pilot to reselect the assigned mode and code APPENDIX 6 SOME USEFUL Q-CODES QBD = Fuel endurance QBI = Compulsory IFR conditions QDL = Do you intend to ask me for a series of bearings? QDM = Magnetic heading to steer to reach station in zero winds QDR = Magnetic bearing fro station QFE = Atmospheric pressure at aerodrome elevation (or runway threshold) or barometric pressure reduced to aerodrome elevation QFU = Magnetic orientation of runway QGE = What is my distance to your station? Or, your distance to my station is …….. QNH = Altimeter sub-scale setting to obtain elevation when on ground QSY = Change of frequency QTE = True bearing from station QBO = Visual meteorological conditions (VMC) exist QUAD = Quadrant QUJ = Will you indicate the true track to reach you? Or, the true track to reach me is ………

text: APPENDIX 7 OPERATION IN THE NAIROBI CONTROL ZONE The Nairobi Control Zone is divided into three areas: (See attached Diagram) (a) AREA I: Ground to 9000 AMSL - Control Authority: (i) Wilson Tower when military zone is not active (ii) Eastleigh Tower when military zone is active; or (iii) Nairobi Approach when Wilson is closed (b) AREA II: Ground to 6500 AMSL - Control Authority: (i) Wilson Tower; and (ii) Nairobi Approach when Wilson AD is closed. (NB: Terrain clearance not provided in AREA II) (c) AREA III: Ground to 9000 AMSL and also area above AREA II to 9000 AMSL - Control Authority: Nairobi Approach. 11.7 VFR Routes 11.7.1 NE Access Lane: (i) Between Eastleigh and JKI Aerodromes. (ii) Track 0690 M from Wilson to ABM THR 06 HJK then 0560 M to Ndula Marker. (iii) Outbound aircraft to fly at 6200 ft & inbound at 6500 ft. AMSL (iv) Pilot must request permission form JKIA Approach frequency 119.7 MHz. 11.7.2 SE Access Lane: (i) Track 324° M from twin Bridges to Visual markers (4.6NM from Wilson bearing 196°M) (ii) Outbound fly at 6200 ft and inbounds at 6,500 ft AMSL (iii) Clearance must be obtained form Nairobi Approach on 119.7 MHz.
